title: Mining
point: Mining is the process of extracting valuable minerals or geological materials from the earth.

title: Mining Sequence
point: Mining sequence is the ordered set of stages involved in mining.

title: Mining Sequence
point: Mining sequence includes prospecting analysis extraction and land restoration.

title: Geology
point: Understanding geology is essential for mine development.

title: Ore
point: Ore is an aggregation of minerals from which metal can be recovered economically.

title: Ore Condition
point: A rock ceases to be ore when valuable content becomes too low for profitable recovery.

title: Mineral
point: A mineral is a naturally occurring chemical compound with definite composition and crystal structure.

title: Mineral Processing
point: Physical properties of minerals govern economic ore processing.

title: Physical Properties
point: Transparency is a physical property of minerals.

title: Physical Properties
point: Luster is a physical property of minerals.

title: Physical Properties
point: Colour is a physical property of minerals.

title: Physical Properties
point: Specific gravity is a physical property of minerals.

title: Physical Properties
point: Hardness is a physical property of minerals.

title: Physical Properties
point: Cleavage fracture magnetic electrical radioactive and optical properties are mineral properties.

title: Transparency
point: Transparency describes how easily light passes through a mineral.

title: Opaque Minerals
point: Opaque minerals do not transmit visible light.

title: Transparent Minerals
point: Transparent minerals can be seen through clearly.

title: Translucent Minerals
point: Translucent minerals allow only partial light transmission.

title: Luster
point: Luster is the amount and quality of light reflected from a mineral surface.

title: Colour
point: Colour results from selective absorption of light wavelengths.

title: Luminescence
point: Luminescence is emission of light not caused by incandescence.

title: Fluorescent Minerals
point: Minerals glowing under UV light or X rays are fluorescent.

title: Specific Gravity
point: Specific gravity of a mineral is nearly constant.

title: Specific Gravity Use
point: Difference in specific gravity helps separate minerals.

title: Gold Separation
point: Gold grains can be separated from quartz sand using gravity differences.

title: Coal Separation
point: Heavy liquids separate lighter coal from heavier shale.

title: Gravity Concentration
point: Gravity concentration is based on differences in specific gravity.

title: Hardness
point: Hardness is the ability of a mineral to resist scratching.

title: Hardness vs Strength
point: Hardness differs from resistance to breaking.

title: Diamond
point: Diamond is extremely hard but can shatter.

title: Mohs Scale
point: Mohs Scale measures relative mineral hardness from 1 to 10.

title: Mohs Creator
point: Mohs scale was developed by Austrian mineralogist F Mohs.

title: Mohs Rank 1
point: Talc has hardness 1.

title: Talc
point: Talc is associated with talcum powder.

title: Mohs Rank 2
point: Gypsum has hardness 2.

title: Gypsum
point: Gypsum is used in plaster of Paris.

title: Gypsum Formation
point: Gypsum forms when seawater evaporates.

title: Mohs Rank 3
point: Calcite has hardness 3.

title: Calcite
point: Calcite occurs in limestone and shells.

title: Mohs Rank 4
point: Fluorite has hardness 4.

title: Fluorite
point: Fluorine in fluorite helps prevent tooth decay.

title: Mohs Rank 5
point: Apatite has hardness 5.

title: Apatite
point: Apatite aids calcium absorption according to the chapter.

title: Mohs Rank 6
point: Orthoclase feldspar has hardness 6.

title: Orthoclase
point: Orthoclase belongs to the feldspar group.

title: Mohs Rank 7
point: Quartz has hardness 7.

title: Quartz
point: Quartz is the most common mineral on Earths surface.

title: Quartz
point: Quartz is natural silicon dioxide.

title: Mohs Rank 8
point: Topaz has hardness 8.

title: Topaz
point: Topaz is the November birthstone.

title: Mohs Rank 9
point: Corundum has hardness 9.

title: Corundum
point: Ruby and sapphire are varieties of corundum.

title: Mohs Rank 10
point: Diamond has hardness 10.

title: Diamond Use
point: Diamond is used in jewellery and cutting tools.

title: Exploration
point: Exploration determines ore body thickness and quality.

title: Core Drilling
point: Exploration commonly uses boreholes and core drilling.

title: Core Recovery
point: Recovered core is preserved carefully in core boxes.

title: Logging
point: Systematic recording of drill cores is called logging.

title: Logging Data
point: Physical properties are recorded in the logbook.

title: Laboratory Analysis
point: Ore and coal samples are analyzed in laboratories.

title: Database
point: Exploration data forms geological and assay databases.

title: Reserve Estimation
point: Geological databases support reserve estimation.

title: Ore Modelling
point: Exploration data is used for ore modelling.

title: Mine Scheduling
point: Exploration supports mine scheduling and quality control.

title: Bench Wise Excavation Plan
point: Bench wise plans target production and quality.

title: Blending
point: Ore from different benches can be blended for desired quality.

title: Bench Parameters
point: Bench height width and gradient must follow DGMS permission.

title: Mine Surface Plan
point: Mine surface plan depicts topography and surface features.

title: Excavation Planning
point: Excavation plans are normally prepared monthly.

title: Mining Scheme
point: Annual excavation follows the approved mining scheme.

title: IBM Permission
point: Annual quantity cannot exceed mining scheme limits without IBM approval.

title: Long Term Plan
point: Long term plans align with mine plan and mining scheme.

title: Progressive Mine Closure Plan
point: Closure planning aims to restore land as close as possible to original form.

title: Mine Plan
point: Mine plan is systematic excavation planning for the lease period.

title: Mine Plan Duration
point: Mine plans are normally prepared for 20 years.

title: Mine Plan Objectives
point: Environmental protection and mineral conservation are key objectives.

title: Mining Scheme
point: A mining scheme accommodates deviations from the original mine plan.

title: Mining Scheme Cycle
point: Mining schemes are prepared every 5 years.

title: Vision 2025
point: Vision 2025 targeted 100 million tonnes of steel production in India.

title: Short Term Plan
point: Short term plans cover annual and monthly excavation activities.

title: Waste Dump
point: Waste dump is stacking of uneconomic waste material.

title: Waste Dump Location
point: Waste dumps should be placed on non mineralized firm ground.

title: Waste Dump Planning
point: Rehandling of waste should be minimized.

title: Hill Slope Dumping
point: Dumping on hill slopes is generally not permitted.

title: Backfilling
point: Old mined areas should be backfilled where possible.

title: Dump Height
point: Waste dumps may be developed in 30 metre segments.

title: Angle of Repose
point: Typical waste dump angle of repose is 33.5 degrees from vertical.

title: Dump Monitoring
point: Dumps are monitored by mining supervisory personnel.

title: Berm Construction
point: Berms should be provided along dump edges.

title: Dust Control
point: Regular water sprinkling suppresses dust.

title: Rainwater Control
point: Waste should not enter rivers or natural watercourses.

title: Topsoil Management
point: Topsoil should be stored separately for rehabilitation.

title: Illumination
point: Minimum illumination level is 3.5 lux during night operations.

title: Equipment Selection
point: Equipment selection depends on ore body size shape and reserves.

title: Selective Mining
point: Erratic deposits may require manual mining.

title: Large Deposits
point: Large deposits favor high capacity equipment.

title: Equipment Combination
point: Excavator dumper combination is commonly used.

title: Equipment Combination
point: In pit crusher belt conveyor combination is used in some mines.

title: Equipment Combination
point: Bucket wheel excavators are used in soft formations.

title: Equipment Combination
point: Draglines are used for overburden removal.

title: Bucket Wheel Excavator
point: It is a continuous high capacity excavator.

title: Dragline
point: Dragline is a giant excavator capable of side casting.

title: Excavator Types
point: Rope shovels and hydraulic excavators are common.

title: Dumper Capacities
point: Dumpers may range from 35 tonnes to 120 tonnes.

title: Support Equipment
point: Dozers graders and water sprinklers support mining.

title: Manufacturers
point: BEML Caterpillar TELCON L&T Komatsu and Atlas Copco are major manufacturers.

title: Dust Suppression
point: Dust suppression is essential for mine operations.

title: Drilling Block
point: Drilling blocks should support at least one week of production.

title: Burden
point: Burden is the distance from free face to first row of holes.

title: Spacing
point: Spacing is the distance between holes in a row.

title: Sub Grade Drilling
point: Sub grade drilling is drilling below bench height.

title: Hole Marking
point: Drill holes are physically marked before drilling.

title: Hard Rock Drilling
point: Hard rock requires smaller burden and spacing.

title: Soft Rock Drilling
point: Soft rock permits larger burden and spacing.

title: Burden Rule
point: Burden equals about 200 to 300 times hole diameter.

title: Spacing Rule
point: Spacing is approximately 1.2 times burden.

title: Subgrade Rule
point: Sub grade drilling is about 10 percent of bench height.

title: Drill Patterns
point: Drill holes may follow square or staggered patterns.

title: Square Pattern
point: Rows align vertically to form squares.

title: Staggered Pattern
point: Second row holes lie between first row holes.

title: Hole Length
point: Hole length should exceed bench height by at least 10 percent.

title: Toes Formation
point: Insufficient drilling creates toes at bench foot.

title: Blasting
point: Blasting fragments rock for excavation.

title: Blasting Importance
point: Blasting efficiency affects all downstream operations.

title: Explosive Types
point: High prime charge is an explosive charge category.

title: Explosive Types
point: Prime charge is an explosive charge category.

title: Explosive Types
point: Base charge is an explosive charge category.

title: Explosive Types
point: Column charge is an explosive charge category.

title: Explosive Types
point: SME explosive is a bulk emulsion explosive.

title: Explosive
point: An explosive rapidly decomposes after ignition.

title: Explosion Effect
point: Explosives release heat and high pressure gases.

title: Blast Effects
point: Rock fragmentation is a blasting effect.

title: Blast Effects
point: Rock displacement is a blasting effect.

title: Blast Effects
point: Ground vibration is a blasting effect.

title: Blast Effects
point: Air blast is a blasting effect.

title: Site Mixed Explosive
point: SME is an on site mixed emulsion explosive.

title: SME Composition
point: SME contains ammonium nitrate water emulsifier oil phase and sensitizer.

title: SME
point: Easier transport handling and storage.

title: SME
point: Provides accurate explosive consumption count.

title: SME
point: Offers shorter charging times.

title: SME
point: Provides excellent water resistance.

title: Blasting Circuit
point: Blasting circuit controls initiation sequence and timing.

title: Detonating Fuse
point: Plain detonators are commonly connected with detonating fuse.

title: Delay Timing
point: Typical row delays are 17 25 or 42 milliseconds.

title: Free Face
point: Creating a free face improves fragmentation.

title: Electric Detonator
point: Electric detonators use delay circuits and an exploder.

title: Electric Detonators
point: Electric systems are vulnerable to stray currents.

title: NONEL
point: NONEL is a non electric shock tube initiation system.

title: NONEL Safety
point: NONEL is largely immune to stray electrical currents.

title: NONEL
point: Allows hole to hole delays.

title: NONEL
point: Hole to hole delays improve fragmentation.

title: NONEL
point: Produces lower vibration.

title: NONEL
point: Produces less rock throw.

title: NONEL
point: Improves muck pile formation.

title: NONEL
point: Reduces chances of misfire.

title: PESO Guideline
point: Electric detonators are being phased out.

title: Deadline
point: Elimination target for electric detonators is before 30 June 2025.

title: Electronic Detonator
point: Electronic detonators provide highly precise delays.

title: Operation Schedule
point: Operation schedules synchronize quality and production targets.

title: Monitoring
point: Bench wise shovel deployment must be monitored.

title: Transport
point: Transport arrangements are vital for men and materials.

title: Quality Control
point: Ore winning should begin after blasting.

title: Quality Control
point: Extraneous materials should be removed before blasting.

title: Primary Crusher
point: Primary crusher is usually a gyratory crusher.

title: Jaw Crusher
point: Jaw crushers may also be used as primary crushers.

title: Primary Crushing Size
point: Primary crushing reduces ore to about 300 mm.

title: Secondary Crusher
point: Secondary crushing commonly uses cone crushers.

title: Secondary Crushing Size
point: Secondary crushing produces material of 40 mm and below.

title: Screening Plant
point: Screening separates lumps and fines.

title: Lump Size
point: Lumps are typically minus 40 mm plus 10 mm.

title: Fine Size
point: Fines are below 10 mm.

title: Wet Screening
point: Wet screening washes ore during monsoon.

title: Beneficiation
point: Beneficiation improves ore quality by removing impurities.

title: Impurities
point: Silica and alumina are removed during beneficiation.

title: Hydrocyclone
point: Hydrocyclones are key beneficiation equipment.

title: Magnetic Separator
point: Magnetic separators recover iron ore fines.

title: Ore Handling Plant
point: Ore handling plant feeds wagon loaders.

title: Bucket Wheel Reclaimer
point: Bucket wheel excavators reclaim stockpiled ore.

title: Loading Challenge
point: Avoiding demurrage is a major dispatch challenge.

title: Loading Accuracy
point: Correct wagon loading avoids penal freight.

title: Mine Safety
point: Safety of workers and equipment is a statutory responsibility.

title: DGMS
point: DGMS provides mine safety guidelines.

title: Statutory Personnel
point: Mining mates foremen managers and engineers ensure safety.

title: Mining Hazards
point: Blasting transportation and electricity pose major hazards.

title: PPE
point: All mine workers must wear personal protective equipment.

title: Dust Control
point: Airborne dust should be suppressed at its source.

title: Water Sprinkling
point: Water sprinkling is used for dust suppression.

title: Equipment Safety
point: Equipment must be kept at safe distances during blasting.

title: Protection Goal
point: Safe distances prevent damage from flying fragments.

title: Exam Recall
point: Mohs Scale order: Talc Gypsum Calcite Fluorite Apatite Orthoclase Quartz Topaz Corundum Diamond.
